securityLevel.description: The hardware backing a signing key. Always the ACHIEVED level, read back from the created key, never assumed from the request. Signet is hardware-only: it fails closed (throws unavailableTier) when no secure hardware is reachable and never produces a software-backed key.

tierEvidence.description: How the achieved SecurityLevel was determined. Both values are on-device self-reports read from the created key (KeyInfo readback on Android, Secure Enclave token presence on Apple), not remote cryptographic proof.

partialOrder.description: The order over tier CLASSES that the atLeast(HardwareClass) floor enforces. discreteSecure ranks above trustedEnvironment; a floor is met by an equal-or-higher class and otherwise fails closed (throws unavailableTier).
